definition:
# ID: enterprise-beans
# DESCRIPTION: Enterprise architecture using YAML bean definitions.
#   This will render a stunning Class Diagram visually!

- beans:
    - name: "orderService"
      type: "com.enterprise.services.OrderService"
      properties:
        auditService: "#auditService"
        maxRetries: 5
        env: "PRODUCTION"
    - name: "auditService"
      type: "com.enterprise.services.AuditService"
      properties:
        dbSource: "#dataSource"
    - name: "dataSource"
      type: "org.apache.commons.dbcp2.BasicDataSource"
      properties:
        url: "jdbc:postgresql://db.corp:5432/orders"
        username: "camel"
        password: "secret"

# ID: dead-letter-channel
# ENABLED: true
# DESCRIPTION: Dead Letter Channel pattern — capture poison messages after max retries
# AUTHOR: Camel Studio
# STUB: stub:jms:queue:DLQ, stub:jms:queue:MAIN.QUEUE

- onException:
    exception:
      - "java.lang.Exception"
    redeliveryPolicy:
      maximumRedeliveries: 3
      redeliveryDelay: 500
    handled:
      constant: true
    steps:
      - log:
          loggingLevel: ERROR
          message: "[Chapter 7][DLC] Dead-lettering message after retries. Cause: ${exception.message}"
      - setHeader:
          name: "X-DLQ-Reason"
          simple: "${exception.message}"
      - setHeader:
          name: "X-DLQ-Timestamp"
          simple: "${date:now:yyyy-MM-dd HH:mm:ss}"
      - to: "{{dlc.dlq.uri}}"

- route:
    id: "main-processor"
    from:
      uri: "{{dlc.main.uri}}"
      steps:
        - log: "[Chapter 7][DLC] Processing message: ${body}"
        - filter:
            simple: "${body} contains 'POISON'"
            steps:
              - throwException:
                  exceptionType: "java.lang.RuntimeException"
                  message: "Poison message detected"
        - to: "mock:processed"

- route:
    id: "dlq-monitor"
    from:
      uri: "{{dlc.dlq.uri}}"
      steps:
        - log: "[Chapter 7][DLC][DLQ] Received dead-letter: ${body} | Reason: ${header.X-DLQ-Reason}"
        - to: "mock:dlq-received"

# ID: transactional-outbox
# ENABLED: true
# DESCRIPTION: Transactional Outbox pattern — poll DB outbox table and publish to Kafka
# AUTHOR: Camel Studio
# STUB: stub:kafka:topic:OUTBOX.EVENTS (replaces real Kafka)

- route:
    id: "outbox-poller"
    from:
      uri: "timer:outbox-poll?period=5000"
      steps:
        - log: "[Chapter 7][OUTBOX] Polling outbox table for unpublished events..."
        - setBody:
            constant: '[{"eventId":"EVT-001","type":"ORDER_CREATED","payload":"{}","status":"PENDING"}]'
        - split:
            jsonpath: "$[*]"
            steps:
              - log: "[Chapter 7][OUTBOX] Publishing event: ${body}"
              - to: "{{outbox.kafka.uri}}"
              - log: "[Chapter 7][OUTBOX] Event published, marking as SENT"
              - to: "mock:outbox-sent"

# ID: saga-pattern
# ENABLED: true
# DESCRIPTION: Saga EIP for distributed transaction coordination with compensation
# AUTHOR: Camel Studio
# STUB: stub:direct:book-hotel, stub:direct:book-flight, stub:direct:charge-payment

- route:
    id: "saga-travel-booking"
    from:
      uri: "timer:saga?period=6000&repeatCount=3"
      steps:
        - setBody:
            constant: '{"tripId":"TRIP-001","customer":"John Doe","destination":"Paris"}'
        - log: "[Chapter 7][SAGA] Starting travel booking saga: ${body}"
        - saga:
            compensation: "direct:cancel-booking"
            completion: "direct:confirm-booking"
            steps:
              - to: "{{saga.hotel.uri}}"
              - log: "[Chapter 7][SAGA] Hotel booked"
              - to: "{{saga.flight.uri}}"
              - log: "[Chapter 7][SAGA] Flight booked"
              - to: "{{saga.payment.uri}}"
              - log: "[Chapter 7][SAGA] Payment charged — saga complete"

- route:
    id: "cancel-booking"
    from:
      uri: "direct:cancel-booking"
      steps:
        - log: "[Chapter 7][SAGA][COMPENSATE] Cancelling booking: ${body}"
        - to: "mock:booking-cancelled"

- route:
    id: "confirm-booking"
    from:
      uri: "direct:confirm-booking"
      steps:
        - log: "[Chapter 7][SAGA][COMPLETE] Booking confirmed: ${body}"
        - to: "mock:booking-confirmed"

# ID: simple-expression
# ENABLED: true
# DESCRIPTION: Uses Simple language to conditionally transform the message body
# AUTHOR: Camel Studio
# STUB: none

- route:
    id: "simple-expression"
    from:
      uri: "timer:tick?period=4000"
      steps:
        - setHeader:
            name: "counter"
            groovy: "(exchange.getProperty('CamelTimerCounter', Integer.class) ?: 0)"
        - setBody:
            simple: "Request #${exchangeProperty.CamelTimerCounter} processed at ${date:now:HH:mm:ss}"
        - log:
            message: "[Chapter 1] ${body}"
        - to: "mock:sink"

# ID: set-body-header
# ENABLED: true
# DESCRIPTION: Demonstrates setBody and setHeader EIPs
# AUTHOR: Camel Studio
# STUB: none

- route:
    id: "set-body-header"
    from:
      uri: "timer:trigger?period=5000&repeatCount=3"
      steps:
        - setHeader:
            name: "MessageType"
            constant: "ORDER"
        - setBody:
            constant: '{"orderId":"ORD-001","amount":199.99,"currency":"USD"}'
        - log:
            message: "[Chapter 1] Header: ${header.MessageType} | Body: ${body}"
        - to: "mock:output"

# ID: hello-timer
# ENABLED: true
# DESCRIPTION: Simplest possible route — timer fires every 3s and logs a message
# AUTHOR: Camel Studio
# STUB: none

- route:
    id: "hello-timer"
    from:
      uri: "timer:hello?period=3000"
      steps:
        - log:
            message: "[Chapter 1] Hello from Apache Camel! Timestamp: ${date:now:HH:mm:ss}"

# ID: multicast
# ENABLED: true
# DESCRIPTION: Multicast EIP — broadcast same message to multiple endpoints in parallel
# AUTHOR: Camel Studio
# STUB: stub:jms:queue:NOTIFY.EMAIL, stub:jms:queue:NOTIFY.SMS

- route:
    id: "multicast-notifier"
    from:
      uri: "timer:notify?period=5000&repeatCount=3"
      steps:
        - setBody:
            constant: '{"event":"PAYMENT_COMPLETE","txId":"TX-12345","amount":499.99}'
        - log: "[Chapter 5][MULTICAST] Broadcasting notification: ${body}"
        - multicast:
            parallelProcessing: true
            steps:
              - to: "{{multicast.email.uri}}"
              - to: "{{multicast.sms.uri}}"
              - to: "mock:audit-trail"

# ID: enrich-pattern
# ENABLED: true
# DESCRIPTION: Content Enricher pattern — fetch extra data and merge into original message
# AUTHOR: Camel Studio
# STUB: stub:direct:fetch-customer (replaces real DB/API call)

- route:
    id: "order-enricher"
    from:
      uri: "timer:enrich?period=4000&repeatCount=4"
      steps:
        - setBody:
            constant: '{"orderId":"ORD-123","customerId":"CUST-42"}'
        - log: "[Chapter 5][ENRICH] Original order: ${body}"
        - enrich:
            expression:
              constant: "{{enrich.customer.uri}}"
            aggregationStrategy: "#class:org.apache.camel.processor.aggregate.GroupedBodyAggregationStrategy"
        - log: "[Chapter 5][ENRICH] Enriched result: ${body}"
        - to: "mock:enriched"

- route:
    id: "customer-service-stub"
    from:
      uri: "{{enrich.customer.uri}}"
      steps:
        - log: "[Chapter 5][ENRICH] Fetching customer for order: ${body}"
        - setBody:
            constant: '{"customerId":"CUST-42","name":"ACME Corp","tier":"GOLD","creditLimit":50000}'

# ID: split-aggregate
# ENABLED: true
# DESCRIPTION: Splitter + Aggregator pattern — split batch, process items, recombine
# AUTHOR: Camel Studio
# STUB: none

- route:
    id: "batch-splitter"
    from:
      uri: "timer:batch?period=6000&repeatCount=3"
      steps:
        - setBody:
            constant: "item1,item2,item3,item4,item5"
        - log: "[Chapter 5][SPLIT] Splitting batch: ${body}"
        - split:
            simple: "${body}"
            delimiter: ","
            steps:
              - log: "[Chapter 5][SPLIT] Processing item: ${body}"
              - setBody:
                  simple: "PROCESSED:${body}:${date:now:HH:mm:ss}"
              - to: "mock:split-item"

# ID: circuit-breaker
# ENABLED: true
# DESCRIPTION: Circuit Breaker pattern protecting against downstream failures with fallback
# AUTHOR: Camel Studio
# STUB: stub:http:api.example.com/orders (replaces live HTTP call)

- route:
    id: "circuit-breaker-demo"
    from:
      uri: "timer:cb-test?period=3000&repeatCount=6"
      steps:
        - log: "[Chapter 4][CB] Calling downstream service..."
        - circuitBreaker:
            steps:
              - setHeader:
                  name: "CamelHttpMethod"
                  constant: "GET"
              - to: "{{downstream.orders.uri}}"
              - log: "[Chapter 4][CB] Success: ${body}"
              - to: "mock:cb-success"
            onFallback:
              steps:
                - log: "[Chapter 4][CB] Circuit OPEN — returning cached fallback"
                - setBody:
                    constant: '{"orders":[],"source":"cache","status":"degraded"}'
                - to: "mock:cb-fallback"

# ID: dotry-docatch
# ENABLED: true
# DESCRIPTION: Inline doTry/doCatch/doFinally for local exception scoping
# AUTHOR: Camel Studio
# STUB: none

- route:
    id: "dotry-example"
    from:
      uri: "timer:try?period=4000&repeatCount=4"
      steps:
        - setBody:
            simple: "${random(1,10)}"
        - doTry:
            steps:
              - log: "[Chapter 4][TRY] Processing value: ${body}"
              - filter:
                  simple: "${body} > 7"
                  steps:
                    - throwException:
                        exceptionType: "java.lang.RuntimeException"
                        message: "Value too large: ${body}"
              - log: "[Chapter 4][TRY] Success: ${body}"
              - to: "mock:success"
            doCatch:
              - exception:
                  - "java.lang.RuntimeException"
                steps:
                  - log: "[Chapter 4][CATCH] Caught exception: ${exception.message}"
                  - to: "mock:caught"
            doFinally:
              steps:
                - log: "[Chapter 4][FINALLY] Cleanup complete"

# ID: global-exception-handler
# ENABLED: true
# DESCRIPTION: Global onException with retry, exponential backoff, and dead-letter channel
# AUTHOR: Camel Studio
# STUB: stub:jms:queue:DLQ (dead letter queue)

- onException:
    exception:
      - "java.io.IOException"
      - "java.net.ConnectException"
    redeliveryPolicy:
      maximumRedeliveries: 3
      redeliveryDelay: 1000
      backOffMultiplier: 2.0
      useExponentialBackOff: true
    handled:
      constant: true
    steps:
      - log:
          loggingLevel: ERROR
          message: "[Chapter 4][DLQ] Routing to dead-letter after ${header.CamelRedeliveryCounter} retries: ${exception.message}"
      - to: "{{dlq.uri}}"

- route:
    id: "flaky-downstream"
    from:
      uri: "timer:errors?period=3000&repeatCount=5"
      steps:
        - setBody:
            simple: "Payload-${random(1,100)}"
        - log: "[Chapter 4] Attempting to send: ${body}"
        - to: "{{dlq.uri}}"
        - log: "[Chapter 4] Sent successfully"

# ID: mongo-audit-exclude
# ENABLED: true
# DESCRIPTION: MongoDB Change Stream audit consumer demonstrating exclusion rules and host metadata resolution.
# AUTHOR: Camel Studio
# STUB: stub:mongodb:audit (replaces real database connection)

- route:
    id: "mongo-change-stream-audit"
    from:
      uri: "{{audit.mongodb.uri}}"
      steps:
        - log: "[Chapter 9][AUDIT] Captured mutation payload: ${body}"
        - to: "mock:filtered-audit"

# ID: ibmmq-xa-narayana
# ENABLED: true
# DESCRIPTION: Demonstrates IBM MQ Jakarta 9.4.5.1 Client configuration with JmsPoolXAConnectionFactory.
# AUTHOR: Camel Studio
# STUB: stub:jms:queue:XA.REQUEST

- route:
    id: "ibmmq-xa-consumer"
    from:
      uri: "{{xa.request.uri}}"
      steps:
        - log: "[Chapter 9][XA] Coordinated transaction message: ${body}"
        - to: "mock:xa-processed"

# ID: aes-gcm-properties
# ENABLED: true
# DESCRIPTION: Demonstrates decryption of credentials in properties files using PBKDF2 GCM key derivation.
# AUTHOR: Camel Studio
# STUB: none

- route:
    id: "secure-properties-loader"
    from:
      uri: "timer:secure-props?period=5000&repeatCount=2"
      steps:
        - log: "[Chapter 9] Database Username: ${properties:db.username:admin}"
        - log: "[Chapter 9] Encrypted Password resolved: ${properties:db.password:ENC(g21A/98s/8da12k=)}"

# ID: choice-routing
# ENABLED: true
# DESCRIPTION: Content-Based Router pattern using Choice/When/Otherwise EIPs
# AUTHOR: Camel Studio
# STUB: none

- route:
    id: "cbr-injector"
    from:
      uri: "timer:cbr?period=2000&repeatCount=6"
      steps:
        - setBody:
            simple: "${random(1,4)}"
        - log: "[Chapter 2] Routing message with type: ${body}"
        - to: "direct:cbr-router"

- route:
    id: "cbr-router"
    from:
      uri: "direct:cbr-router"
      steps:
        - choice:
            when:
              - simple: "${body} == '1'"
                steps:
                  - log: "[Chapter 2] >> Priority GOLD order"
                  - to: "mock:gold"
              - simple: "${body} == '2'"
                steps:
                  - log: "[Chapter 2] >> Priority SILVER order"
                  - to: "mock:silver"
              - simple: "${body} == '3'"
                steps:
                  - log: "[Chapter 2] >> Priority BRONZE order"
                  - to: "mock:bronze"
            otherwise:
              steps:
                - log: "[Chapter 2] >> Unknown priority, rejecting"
                - to: "mock:rejected"

# ID: filter-eip
# ENABLED: true
# DESCRIPTION: Message Filter EIP — only passes messages matching the predicate
# AUTHOR: Camel Studio
# STUB: none

- route:
    id: "filter-high-value"
    from:
      uri: "timer:orders?period=2500"
      steps:
        - setBody:
            simple: "${random(100,1000)}"
        - log: "[Chapter 2] Order amount: ${body}"
        - filter:
            simple: "${body} > 500"
            steps:
              - log: "[Chapter 2][FILTER] High-value order detected: ${body}"
              - to: "mock:high-value"

# ID: wiretap-audit
# ENABLED: true
# DESCRIPTION: WireTap pattern — async audit copy without slowing main flow
# AUTHOR: Camel Studio
# STUB: mock (stub replaces JMS audit queue)

- route:
    id: "main-payment-flow"
    from:
      uri: "timer:payment?period=3000&repeatCount=4"
      steps:
        - setBody:
            constant: '{"txId":"TX-${random(1000,9999)}","amount":500}'
        - log: "[Chapter 2] Processing payment: ${body}"
        - wireTap:
            uri: "{{wiretap.audit.uri}}"
        - to: "mock:payment-processed"

- route:
    id: "audit-consumer"
    from:
      uri: "{{wiretap.audit.uri}}"
      steps:
        - log: "[Chapter 2][AUDIT] Captured audit event: ${body}"
        - to: "mock:audit-log"

<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<project xmlns="http://maven.apache.org/POM/4.0.0" xmlns:xsi="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance"
    xsi:schemaLocation="http://maven.apache.org/POM/4.0.0 https://maven.apache.org/xsd/maven-4.0.0.xsd">
    <modelVersion>4.0.0</modelVersion>

    <groupId>org.example.project</groupId>
    <artifactId>CamelJBang</artifactId>
    <version>1.0-SNAPSHOT</version>

    <properties>
        <project.build.outputTimestamp>2026-05-25T16:14:56Z</project.build.outputTimestamp>
        <compiler-plugin.version>3.14.1</compiler-plugin.version>
        <failsafe.useModulePath>false</failsafe.useModulePath>
        <maven.compiler.release>21</maven.compiler.release>
        <project.build.sourceEncoding>UTF-8</project.build.sourceEncoding>
        <project.reporting.outputEncoding>UTF-8</project.reporting.outputEncoding>
        <quarkus.platform.group-id>io.quarkus.platform</quarkus.platform.group-id>
        <quarkus.platform.artifact-id>quarkus-bom</quarkus.platform.artifact-id>
        <quarkus.platform.version>3.33.1.1</quarkus.platform.version>

        <skipITs>true</skipITs>
        <surefire-plugin.version>3.5.4</surefire-plugin.version>
    </properties>

    <dependencyManagement>
        <dependencies>
            <dependency>
                <groupId>${quarkus.platform.group-id}</groupId>
                <artifactId>${quarkus.platform.artifact-id}</artifactId>
                <version>${quarkus.platform.version}</version>
                <type>pom</type>
                <scope>import</scope>
            </dependency>
            <dependency>
                <groupId>${quarkus.platform.group-id}</groupId>
                <artifactId>quarkus-camel-bom</artifactId>
                <version>${quarkus.platform.version}</version>
                <type>pom</type>
                <scope>import</scope>
            </dependency>
        </dependencies>
    </dependencyManagement>



    <dependencies>
        <dependency>
            <groupId>org.apache.camel.quarkus</groupId>
            <artifactId>camel-quarkus-core</artifactId>
        </dependency>
        <dependency>
            <groupId>org.apache.camel.quarkus</groupId>
            <artifactId>camel-quarkus-cli-connector</artifactId>
        </dependency>
        <dependency>
            <groupId>org.apache.camel.quarkus</groupId>
            <artifactId>camel-quarkus-jackson</artifactId>
        </dependency>
        <dependency>
            <groupId>org.apache.camel.quarkus</groupId>
            <artifactId>camel-quarkus-platform-http</artifactId>
        </dependency>
        <dependency>
            <groupId>org.apache.camel.quarkus</groupId>
            <artifactId>camel-quarkus-yaml-dsl</artifactId>
        </dependency>


        <dependency>
            <groupId>io.quarkus</groupId>
            <artifactId>quarkus-junit5</artifactId>
            <scope>test</scope>
        </dependency>
    </dependencies>

    <build>
        <plugins>
            <plugin>
                <groupId>${quarkus.platform.group-id}</groupId>
                <artifactId>quarkus-maven-plugin</artifactId>
                <version>${quarkus.platform.version}</version>
                <extensions>true</extensions>
                <executions>
                    <execution>
                        <goals>
                            <goal>build</goal>
                            <goal>generate-code</goal>
                            <goal>generate-code-tests</goal>
                        </goals>
                    </execution>
                </executions>
            </plugin>
            <plugin>
                <artifactId>maven-compiler-plugin</artifactId>
                <version>${compiler-plugin.version}</version>
                <configuration>
                    <compilerArgs>
                        <arg>-parameters</arg>
                    </compilerArgs>
                </configuration>
            </plugin>
            <plugin>
                <artifactId>maven-surefire-plugin</artifactId>
                <version>${surefire-plugin.version}</version>
                <configuration>
                    <systemPropertyVariables>
                        <java.util.logging.manager>org.jboss.logmanager.LogManager</java.util.logging.manager>
                        <maven.home>${maven.home}</maven.home>
                    </systemPropertyVariables>
                </configuration>
            </plugin>
            <plugin>
                <artifactId>maven-failsafe-plugin</artifactId>
                <version>${surefire-plugin.version}</version>
                <executions>
                    <execution>
                        <goals>
                            <goal>integration-test</goal>
                            <goal>verify</goal>
                        </goals>
                        <configuration>
                            <systemPropertyVariables>
                                <native.image.path>${project.build.directory}/${project.build.finalName}-runner</native.image.path>
                                <java.util.logging.manager>org.jboss.logmanager.LogManager</java.util.logging.manager>
                                <maven.home>${maven.home}</maven.home>
                            </systemPropertyVariables>
                        </configuration>
                    </execution>
                </executions>
            </plugin>
        </plugins>
    </build>

    <profiles>
        <profile>
            <id>native</id>
            <activation>
                <property>
                    <name>native</name>
                </property>
            </activation>
            <properties>
                <skipITs>false</skipITs>
                <quarkus.package.type>native</quarkus.package.type>
            </properties>
        </profile>
        <profile>
            <id>camel.debug</id>
            <activation>
                <property>
                    <name>camel.debug</name>
                    <value>true</value>
                </property>
            </activation>
            <dependencies>
                <dependency>
                    <groupId>org.apache.camel.quarkus</groupId>
                    <artifactId>camel-quarkus-debug</artifactId>
                </dependency>
            </dependencies>
            <build>
                <plugins>
                    <plugin>
                        <groupId>org.apache.maven.plugins</groupId>
                        <artifactId>maven-dependency-plugin</artifactId>
                        <version>3.8.1</version>
                        <executions>
                            <execution>
                                <id>copy</id>
                                <phase>generate-sources</phase>
                                <goals>
                                    <goal>copy</goal>
                                </goals>
                                <configuration>
                                    <artifactItems>
                                      <artifactItem>
                                        <groupId>org.jolokia</groupId>
                                        <artifactId>jolokia-agent-jvm</artifactId>
                                        <version>2.3.0</version>
                                        <type>jar</type>
                                        <classifier>javaagent</classifier>
                                      </artifactItem>
                                    </artifactItems>
                                    <stripVersion>true</stripVersion>
                                </configuration>
                            </execution>
                        </executions>
                    </plugin>
                    <plugin>
                        <groupId>${quarkus.platform.group-id}</groupId>
                        <artifactId>quarkus-maven-plugin</artifactId>
                        <version>${quarkus.platform.version}</version>
                        <configuration>
                            <jvmArgs>-Dcamel.main.shutdownTimeout=30 -Dquarkus.camel.source-location-enabled=true -javaagent:target/dependency/jolokia-agent-jvm-javaagent.jar=port=7878,host=localhost</jvmArgs>
                        </configuration>
                    </plugin>
                </plugins>
            </build>
        </profile>
    </profiles>

</project>

# ID: rest-json-transform
# ENABLED: true
# DESCRIPTION: REST endpoint that accepts a raw payment object and transforms it
#   to a canonical format using setBody + Simple expressions.
#
#   curl -X POST http://localhost:8080/api/payments \
#     -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
#     -d '{"sender":"Alice","receiver":"Bob","amount":250.00,"currency":"USD"}'
# AUTHOR: Camel Studio
# STUB: none — live HTTP server

- route:
    id: "payment-transformer"
    from:
      uri: "platform-http:/api/payments"
      parameters:
        httpMethodRestrict: "POST"
      steps:
        - log: "[Chapter 8][PAY] Received payment: ${body}"
        - unmarshal:
            json:
              library: Jackson
        - setHeader:
            name: "txRef"
            simple: "TXN-${random(100000,999999)}"
        - setHeader:
            name: "Content-Type"
            constant: "application/json"
        - marshal:
            json:
              library: Jackson
        - log: "[Chapter 8][PAY] Canonical payment dispatched: ref=${header.txRef}"
        - setBody:
            simple: '{"txRef":"${header.txRef}","status":"ACCEPTED","timestamp":"${date:now:yyyy-MM-dd HH:mm:ss"}'
        - setHeader:
            name: "CamelHttpResponseCode"
            constant: "202"

# ID: rest-json-transform
# ENABLED: true
# DESCRIPTION: REST endpoint that accepts a raw payment object and transforms it
#   to a canonical format using setBody + Simple expressions.
#
#   curl -X POST http://localhost:8080/api/payments \
#     -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
#     -d '{"sender":"Alice","receiver":"Bob","amount":250.00,"currency":"USD"}'
# AUTHOR: Camel Studio
# STUB: none — live HTTP server

- route:
    id: "payment-transformer"
    from:
      uri: "platform-http:/api/payments"
      parameters:
        httpMethodRestrict: "POST"
      steps:
        - log: "[Chapter 8][PAY] Received payment: ${body}"
        - unmarshal:
            json:
              library: Jackson
        - setHeader:
            name: "txRef"
            simple: "TXN-${random(100000,999999)}"
        - setHeader:
            name: "Content-Type"
            constant: "application/json"
        - marshal:
            json:
              library: Jackson
        - log: "[Chapter 8][PAY] Canonical payment dispatched: ref=${header.txRef}"
        - setBody:
            simple: '{"txRef":"${header.txRef}","status":"ACCEPTED","timestamp":"${date:now:yyyy-MM-dd HH:mm:ss"}'
        - setHeader:
            name: "CamelHttpResponseCode"
            constant: "202"

# ID: rest-json-transform
# ENABLED: true
# DESCRIPTION: REST endpoint that accepts a raw payment object and transforms it
#   to a canonical format using setBody + Simple expressions.
#
#   curl -X POST http://localhost:8080/api/payments \
#     -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
#     -d '{"sender":"Alice","receiver":"Bob","amount":250.00,"currency":"USD"}'
# AUTHOR: Camel Studio
# STUB: none — live HTTP server

- route:
    id: "payment-transformer"
    from:
      uri: "platform-http:/api/payments"
      parameters:
        httpMethodRestrict: "POST"
      steps:
        - log: "[Chapter 8][PAY] Received payment: ${body}"
        - unmarshal:
            json:
              library: Jackson
        - setHeader:
            name: "txRef"
            simple: "TXN-${random(100000,999999)}"
        - setHeader:
            name: "Content-Type"
            constant: "application/json"
        - marshal:
            json:
              library: Jackson
        - log: "[Chapter 8][PAY] Canonical payment dispatched: ref=${header.txRef}"
        - setBody:
            simple: '{"txRef":"${header.txRef}","status":"ACCEPTED","timestamp":"${date:now:yyyy-MM-dd HH:mm:ss"}'
        - setHeader:
            name: "CamelHttpResponseCode"
            constant: "202"

# ID: platform-http-hello
# ENABLED: true
# DESCRIPTION: Simplest live HTTP endpoint using platform-http component.
#   Starts a real server on port 8080. Run this file then call:
#     curl http://localhost:8080/hello
#     curl "http://localhost:8080/hello?name=Pratyush"
# AUTHOR: Camel Studio
# STUB: none — live HTTP server

- route:
    id: "http-hello"
    from:
      uri: "platform-http:/hello"
      parameters:
        httpMethodRestrict: "GET"
      steps:
        - setBody:
            simple: "Hello, ${header.name}! Welcome to Camel REST on Quarkus."
        - log: "[Chapter 8][HELLO] Served: ${body}"
        - setHeader:
            name: "Content-Type"
            constant: "text/plain"

# ID: rest-health-metrics
# ENABLED: true
# DESCRIPTION: Health-check and metrics endpoints (standard in microservices).
#
#   curl http://localhost:8080/health
#   curl http://localhost:8080/health/live
#   curl http://localhost:8080/health/ready
#   curl http://localhost:8080/metrics
#
# AUTHOR: Camel Studio
# STUB: none — live HTTP server

- route:
    id: "health-overall"
    from:
      uri: "platform-http:/health"
      parameters:
        httpMethodRestrict: "GET"
      steps:
        - setHeader:
            name: "Content-Type"
            constant: "application/json"
        - setBody:
            simple: '{"status":"UP","timestamp":"${date:now:yyyy-MM-dd HH:mm:ss}","components":{"camel":{"status":"UP"},"db":{"status":"UP"}}}'

- route:
    id: "health-live"
    from:
      uri: "platform-http:/health/live"
      parameters:
        httpMethodRestrict: "GET"
      steps:
        - setHeader:
            name: "Content-Type"
            constant: "application/json"
        - setBody:
            constant: '{"status":"UP"}'

- route:
    id: "health-ready"
    from:
      uri: "platform-http:/health/ready"
      parameters:
        httpMethodRestrict: "GET"
      steps:
        - setHeader:
            name: "Content-Type"
            constant: "application/json"
        - setBody:
            constant: '{"status":"UP","checks":[{"name":"camel-context","status":"UP"}]}'

- route:
    id: "metrics-endpoint"
    from:
      uri: "platform-http:/metrics"
      parameters:
        httpMethodRestrict: "GET"
      steps:
        - setHeader:
            name: "Content-Type"
            constant: "text/plain"
        - setBody:
            simple: "# HELP camel_routes_running_routes Number of running routes\n# TYPE camel_routes_running_routes gauge\ncamel_routes_running_routes 4\n# HELP camel_exchanges_total Total exchanges processed\n# TYPE camel_exchanges_total counter\ncamel_exchanges_total ${exchangeProperty.CamelTimerCounter}\n"

# ID: rest-auth-header
# ENABLED: true
# DESCRIPTION: REST endpoint that validates an API-Key header before serving.
#   curl -H 'X-API-Key: secret123' http://localhost:8080/secure/data
#   curl http://localhost:8080/secure/data   <- returns 401
# AUTHOR: Camel Studio
# STUB: none — live HTTP server

- route:
    id: "secure-endpoint"
    from:
      uri: "platform-http:/secure/data"
      parameters:
        httpMethodRestrict: "GET"
      steps:
        - choice:
            when:
              - simple: "${header.X-API-Key} == 'secret123'"
                steps:
                  - log: "[Chapter 8][AUTH] Authorized request"
                  - setBody:
                      constant: '{"data":"Sensitive payload","clearance":"TOP_SECRET"}'
                  - setHeader:
                      name: "Content-Type"
                      constant: "application/json"
            otherwise:
              steps:
                - log: "[Chapter 8][AUTH] Unauthorized — missing or wrong API key"
                - setHeader:
                    name: "CamelHttpResponseCode"
                    constant: "401"
                - setBody:
                    constant: '{"error":"Unauthorized","message":"Valid X-API-Key header required"}'

# ID: rest-file-upload
# ENABLED: true
# DESCRIPTION: Multipart file upload endpoint — receives a file and saves it.
#
#   curl -X POST http://localhost:8080/upload \
#     -F 'file=@/path/to/yourfile.xml'
#
# AUTHOR: Camel Studio
# STUB: none — live HTTP server

- route:
    id: "file-upload-handler"
    from:
      uri: "platform-http:/upload"
      parameters:
        httpMethodRestrict: "POST"
      steps:
        - log: "[Chapter 8][UPLOAD] Received upload — Content-Type: ${header.Content-Type}"
        - choice:
            when:
              - simple: "${body} != null"
                steps:
                  - setHeader:
                      name: "CamelFileName"
                      simple: "upload-${date:now:yyyyMMdd-HHmmss}.bin"
                  - to: "file:uploads"
                  - log: "[Chapter 8][UPLOAD] Saved file: ${header.CamelFileName}"
                  - setBody:
                      simple: '{"status":"uploaded","file":"${header.CamelFileName}","size":"${body.length}"}'
            otherwise:
              steps:
                - setHeader:
                    name: "CamelHttpResponseCode"
                    constant: "400"
                - setBody:
                    constant: '{"error":"No file received"}'
        - setHeader:
            name: "Content-Type"
            constant: "application/json"

# ID: rest-proxy-gateway
# ENABLED: true
# DESCRIPTION: API Gateway pattern — receives HTTP requests and proxies them to a
#   downstream service (stubbed here). Adds correlation ID, auth header, logging.
#
#   curl http://localhost:8080/gateway/products
#   curl http://localhost:8080/gateway/products/42
# STUB: stub:http:downstream-service (replaces real downstream)
# AUTHOR: Camel Studio

- route:
    id: "api-gateway"
    from:
      uri: "platform-http:/gateway/{resource}"
      steps:
        - setHeader:
            name: "X-Correlation-ID"
            simple: "COR-${random(100000,999999)}"
        - setHeader:
            name: "X-Gateway-Timestamp"
            simple: "${date:now:yyyy-MM-dd HH:mm:ss}"
        - log: "[Chapter 8][GW] Routing ${header.CamelHttpMethod} /${header.resource} — CorID: ${header.X-Correlation-ID}"
        - circuitBreaker:
            steps:
              - setHeader:
                  name: "Authorization"
                  constant: "Bearer internal-service-token"
              - to: "{{gateway.downstream.uri}}/api/${header.resource}"
              - log: "[Chapter 8][GW] Downstream responded: ${body}"
            onFallback:
              steps:
                - log: "[Chapter 8][GW] Downstream unreachable — returning 503"
                - setHeader:
                    name: "CamelHttpResponseCode"
                    constant: "503"
                - setBody:
                    constant: '{"error":"Service Unavailable","retryAfter":30}'
        - setHeader:
            name: "Content-Type"
            constant: "application/json"

# ID: rest-dsl-crud
# ENABLED: true
# DESCRIPTION: Full CRUD REST API using the Camel REST DSL.
#   Starts a live HTTP server on port 8080.
#
#   CREATE:  curl -X POST http://localhost:8080/api/orders \
#              -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
#              -d '{"product":"Widget","qty":5}'
#
#   READ:    curl http://localhost:8080/api/orders/ORD-001
#
#   UPDATE:  curl -X PUT http://localhost:8080/api/orders/ORD-001 \
#              -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
#              -d '{"qty":10}'
#
#   DELETE:  curl -X DELETE http://localhost:8080/api/orders/ORD-001
#
#   LIST:    curl http://localhost:8080/api/orders
# AUTHOR: Camel Studio
# STUB: none — live HTTP server

- rest:
    path: "/api/orders"
    consumes: "application/json"
    produces: "application/json"
    get:
      - path: "/"
        description: "List all orders"
        to: "direct:list-orders"
      - path: "/{id}"
        description: "Get order by ID"
        to: "direct:get-order"
    post:
      - path: "/"
        description: "Create a new order"
        to: "direct:create-order"
    put:
      - path: "/{id}"
        description: "Update an order"
        to: "direct:update-order"
    delete:
      - path: "/{id}"
        description: "Delete an order"
        to: "direct:delete-order"

- route:
    id: "list-orders"
    from:
      uri: "direct:list-orders"
      steps:
        - log: "[Chapter 8][CRUD] GET /api/orders"
        - setBody:
            constant: '[{"id":"ORD-001","product":"Widget","qty":5,"status":"ACTIVE"},{"id":"ORD-002","product":"Gadget","qty":2,"status":"PENDING"}]'

- route:
    id: "get-order"
    from:
      uri: "direct:get-order"
      steps:
        - log: "[Chapter 8][CRUD] GET /api/orders/${header.id}"
        - setBody:
            simple: '{"id":"${header.id}","product":"Widget","qty":5,"status":"ACTIVE","createdAt":"${date:now:yyyy-MM-dd HH:mm:ss"}'

- route:
    id: "create-order"
    from:
      uri: "direct:create-order"
      steps:
        - log: "[Chapter 8][CRUD] POST /api/orders body=${body}"
        - setHeader:
            name: "newOrderId"
            simple: "ORD-${random(1000,9999)}"
        - setBody:
            simple: '{"id":"${header.newOrderId}","status":"CREATED","createdAt":"${date:now:yyyy-MM-dd HH:mm:ss"}'
        - setHeader:
            name: "CamelHttpResponseCode"
            constant: "201"

- route:
    id: "update-order"
    from:
      uri: "direct:update-order"
      steps:
        - log: "[Chapter 8][CRUD] PUT /api/orders/${header.id} body=${body}"
        - setBody:
            simple: '{"id":"${header.id}","status":"UPDATED","updatedAt":"${date:now:yyyy-MM-dd HH:mm:ss"}'

- route:
    id: "delete-order"
    from:
      uri: "direct:delete-order"
      steps:
        - log: "[Chapter 8][CRUD] DELETE /api/orders/${header.id}"
        - setBody:
            simple: '{"id":"${header.id}","status":"DELETED"}'
        - setHeader:
            name: "CamelHttpResponseCode"
            constant: "200"

# ID: rest-streaming-sse
# ENABLED: true
# DESCRIPTION: Server-Sent Events (SSE) style streaming endpoint.
#   Pushes a stream of price-tick events to the caller every second.
#
#   curl -N http://localhost:8080/stream/prices
#
# AUTHOR: Camel Studio
# STUB: none — live HTTP server

- route:
    id: "price-feed-producer"
    from:
      uri: "timer:price-tick?period=1000"
      steps:
        - setBody:
            simple: "data: {\"symbol\":\"EUR/USD\",\"price\":\"${random(10800,10900)}\",\"ts\":\"${date:now:HH:mm:ss}\"}\n\n"
        - to: "direct:price-broadcaster"

- route:
    id: "sse-endpoint"
    from:
      uri: "platform-http:/stream/prices"
      parameters:
        httpMethodRestrict: "GET"
      steps:
        - setHeader:
            name: "Content-Type"
            constant: "text/event-stream"
        - setHeader:
            name: "Cache-Control"
            constant: "no-cache"
        - setBody:
            constant: "data: {\"message\":\"Connected to Camel price stream\"}\n\n"
        - log: "[Chapter 8][SSE] Client connected to price stream"

# ID: kafka-consumer-stub
# ENABLED: true
# DESCRIPTION: Consumes messages from a Kafka topic. Uses stub: in offline mode.
# STUB: stub:kafka:topic:orders (replaces kafka:orders?brokers=localhost:9092)
# REAL_URI: kafka:orders?brokers=localhost:9092&groupId=camel-studio

- route:
    id: "kafka-order-consumer"
    from:
      uri: "{{kafka.orders.uri}}"
      steps:
        - log: "[Chapter 3][KAFKA] Received order from Kafka: ${body}"
        - setHeader:
            name: "ProcessedAt"
            simple: "${date:now:yyyy-MM-dd HH:mm:ss}"
        - to: "mock:order-processor"

- route:
    id: "kafka-order-publisher"
    from:
      uri: "timer:kafka-test?period=4000&repeatCount=5"
      steps:
        - setBody:
            simple: '{"orderId":"ORD-${random(100,999)}","product":"Widget","qty":${random(1,10)}}'
        - log: "[Chapter 3][KAFKA] Sending to Kafka: ${body}"
        - to: "{{kafka.orders.uri}}"

# ID: mongodb-stub
# ENABLED: true
# DESCRIPTION: Saves and retrieves documents from MongoDB using stubs offline
# STUB: stub:mongodb:cameldb (replaces mongodb:cameldb?operation=insert&collection=orders)
# REAL_URI: mongodb:myMongoBean?database=cameldb&collection=orders&operation=insert

- route:
    id: "mongodb-insert"
    from:
      uri: "timer:mongo?period=4000&repeatCount=4"
      steps:
        - setBody:
            simple: '{"_id":"${random(1000,9999)}","customer":"ACME Corp","amount":${random(100,5000)},"ts":"${date:now:yyyy-MM-dd}"}'
        - log: "[Chapter 3][MONGO] Inserting document: ${body}"
        - to: "{{mongodb.orders.uri}}"
        - log: "[Chapter 3][MONGO] Insert complete, result: ${body}"
        - to: "mock:mongo-inserted"

# ID: jms-ibmmq-stub
# ENABLED: true
# DESCRIPTION: IBM MQ / JMS request-reply pattern using stubs for offline testing
# STUB: stub:jms:queue:REQUEST.Q and stub:jms:queue:REPLY.Q
# REAL_URI: jms:queue:REQUEST.Q?connectionFactory=#ibmMQFactory

- route:
    id: "ibmmq-request-sender"
    from:
      uri: "timer:mq-test?period=5000&repeatCount=4"
      steps:
        - setBody:
            constant: '<TransactionRequest><TxId>TXN-001</TxId><Amount>1000.00</Amount></TransactionRequest>'
        - setHeader:
            name: "JMSCorrelationID"
            simple: "CID-${random(10000,99999)}"
        - log: "[Chapter 3][IBM-MQ] Sending to request queue: ${header.JMSCorrelationID}"
        - to: "{{ibmmq.request.uri}}"

- route:
    id: "ibmmq-request-handler"
    from:
      uri: "{{ibmmq.request.uri}}"
      steps:
        - log: "[Chapter 3][IBM-MQ] Processing request: ${body}"
        - setBody:
            constant: '<TransactionResponse><Status>APPROVED</Status><Auth>AUTH-789</Auth></TransactionResponse>'
        - to: "{{ibmmq.reply.uri}}"

- route:
    id: "ibmmq-reply-consumer"
    from:
      uri: "{{ibmmq.reply.uri}}"
      steps:
        - log: "[Chapter 3][IBM-MQ] Got reply: ${body}"
        - to: "mock:mq-completed"

# ID: regex-routing
# DESCRIPTION: Routing based on Regular Expressions on headers

- route:
    id: "regex-router"
    from:
      uri: "timer:regex?period=4000"
      steps:
        - setHeader:
            name: "UserAgent"
            simple: "Mozilla/5.0 (iPhone; CPU iPhone OS 15_0)"
        - choice:
            when:
              - simple: "${header.UserAgent} regex '.*(iPhone|Android).*'"
                steps:
                  - log: "Mobile client detected"
            otherwise:
              steps:
                - log: "Desktop client detected"

# ID: nested-choice
# DESCRIPTION: Complex decision tree using nested Choice EIPs

- route:
    id: "decision-tree"
    from:
      uri: "timer:decision?period=5000"
      steps:
        - setBody:
            simple: "${random(1,100)}"
        - choice:
            when:
              - simple: "${body} > 50"
                steps:
                  - choice:
                      when:
                        - simple: "${body} > 80"
                          steps:
                            - log: "CRITICAL: High value detected"
                      otherwise:
                        steps:
                          - log: "WARNING: Medium-high value"
            otherwise:
              steps:
                - log: "INFO: Low value"

# ID: rest-producer
# ENABLED: true
# DESCRIPTION: Calls an external REST API using the http component (stubbed for offline)
# AUTHOR: Camel Studio
# STUB: stub:http:api.example.com (replaces real HTTP)

- route:
    id: "rest-api-caller"
    from:
      uri: "timer:http?period=5000&repeatCount=4"
      steps:
        - setHeader:
            name: "CamelHttpMethod"
            constant: "GET"
        - setHeader:
            name: "Accept"
            constant: "application/json"
        - log: "[Chapter 6][REST] Calling external API..."
        - to: "{{rest.products.uri}}"
        - log: "[Chapter 6][REST] API Response: ${body}"
        - to: "mock:api-result"

# ID: rest-consumer
# ENABLED: true
# DESCRIPTION: Exposes REST endpoints using Camel REST DSL (Quarkus platform)
# AUTHOR: Camel Studio
# STUB: none (self-hosted REST server)

- rest:
    path: "/api/v1"
    post:
      - path: "/orders"
        consumes: "application/json"
        produces: "application/json"
        to: "direct:create-order"
    get:
      - path: "/orders/{id}"
        produces: "application/json"
        to: "direct:get-order"
    delete:
      - path: "/orders/{id}"
        to: "direct:delete-order"

- route:
    id: "create-order"
    from:
      uri: "direct:create-order"
      steps:
        - log: "[Chapter 6][REST] POST /orders - Body: ${body}"
        - setHeader:
            name: "orderId"
            simple: "ORD-${random(1000,9999)}"
        - setBody:
            simple: '{"orderId":"${header.orderId}","status":"CREATED","timestamp":"${date:now:yyyy-MM-dd HH:mm:ss}"}'

- route:
    id: "get-order"
    from:
      uri: "direct:get-order"
      steps:
        - log: "[Chapter 6][REST] GET /orders/${header.id}"
        - setBody:
            simple: '{"orderId":"${header.id}","status":"ACTIVE","amount":299.99}'

- route:
    id: "delete-order"
    from:
      uri: "direct:delete-order"
      steps:
        - log: "[Chapter 6][REST] DELETE /orders/${header.id}"
        - setBody:
            simple: '{"orderId":"${header.id}","status":"DELETED"}'
